symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional central crushing chest pain radiating to the left jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional, central, jaw-radiating pain is a cardinal anginal feature.
Musculoskeletal pain is positional/reproducible, not exertional with jaw radiation.
Reflux relates to meals/posture, not exertion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest pain only partially relieved by rest with a persisting dull ache
symptoms.duration: 20 minutes of severe pain then residual ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest/prolonged pain indicates an unstable coronary syndrome rather than stable angina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Two-week crescendo of exertional chest tightness (new-onset worsening angina)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New-onset, accelerating angina meets the definition of unstable angina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of premature myocardial infarction / sudden cardiac death and markedly elevated cholesterol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Premature family CAD sharply raises pre-test probability of coronary disease.
Autosomal-dominant inheritance — premature MI plus very high family cholesterol is a defining feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised fatigue accompanying the chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue is a non-specific associated symptom that can accompany myocardial ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral Achilles tendon xanthomas (firm nodular tendon swellings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tendon xanthomas are near-pathognomonic of Familial Hypercholesterolaemia. Clinician-palpated — sealed; the patient may report firm ankle lumps but cannot interpret them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Xanthelasma — yellowish cholesterol deposits on both upper eyelids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Xanthelasma, especially in a young person, points to a severe lipid disorder. Video-observable but requires clinical interpretation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral corneal arcus in a young adult

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Corneal arcus before age 45 is abnormal and strongly associated with severe hypercholesterolaemia. Requires slit-lamp/clinical examination — sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated LDL-C (pending formal laboratory confirmation)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Undiagnosed FH and untreated severe hypercholesterolaemia, with continued accelerated atherosclerosis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe LDL elevation is a defining laboratory criterion for FH. Not available in telehealth in real time — sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: May show ischaemic ST/T changes; a normal ECG does not exclude ACS

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG is mandatory in acute chest pain to detect ischaemia. Cannot be obtained via telehealth — requires emergency escalation.

investigation_details.expected_result: Rise/fall pattern would confirm myocardial infarction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serial troponin is required to confirm or exclude MI — only available after hospital escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal heart sounds with no murmur and no signs of heart failure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal auscultation and absence of heart-failure signs do NOT exclude ACS. Clinician-auscultated — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: High anxiety / distress about family history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anxiety is a decoy — it must not be used to explain away exertional cardiac chest pain.
Distress is an appropriate reaction to genuine cardiac symptoms and does not lower cardiac probability.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional jaw-radiating pain + incomplete rest relief + two-week crescendo together define an unstable coronary syndrome requiring emergency assessment.
Tendon xanthomas, xanthelasma, corneal arcus in youth, premature family MI and severe LDL elevation together indicate probable FH driving premature CAD.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory first investigation in acute chest pain to detect ischaemia; obtained only after emergency escalation.
To confirm severe hypercholesterolaemia and support the clinical diagnosis of FH once the acute event is managed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Genetic testing for FH and cascade screening of relatives
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the FH diagnosis and identifies at-risk family members — a specialist lipid-clinic step after discharge, not an acute action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Coronary CT angiography / exercise stress testing in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not the correct acute pathway; an unstable presentation goes to emergency assessment with ECG and troponin, not outpatient stress testing.
